stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 20 factory workers. The sample mean is 112.1 sec with a sample standard deviation of 37.6 sec. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 107.6 sec and standard deviation of 23.0 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 112.1, 95% CI = [94.5, 129.7]. Without outliers: mean = 107.6, 95% CI = [96.8, 118.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [94.5, 129.7]. Point estimate: 109.8 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [94.5, 129.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
